name: HRC_TXN_HEADER_ARCHIVE
description: Transaction Model Archive table: contain the archives of changes being made in this transaction

columns.Name: TRANSACTION_ID
columns.Comments: Primary Key and unique identifier

columns.Name: IS_TXN_GETTING_PURGED
columns.Comments: Flag to indicate if archived transaction is a candidate for getting purged.

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of Enterprise, used for multi-tenancy partitioning. Foreign key to HR_ORGANIZATION_UNITS.

columns.Name: ARCHIVE_STEP_NUMBER
columns.Comments: This records sequence number within the archived transaction

columns.Name: INITIATOR_USER_ID
columns.Comments: The user_id of the user who initiated the transaction

columns.Name: APPLICATION_ID
columns.Comments: The identifier of the Application that set up the business data for this transaction

columns.Name: MODULE_GROUP
columns.Comments: The Module Group of the module that set up the business data

columns.Name: MODULE_IDENTIFIER
columns.Comments: The Module that set up the business data

columns.Name: PROCESS_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key to HRC_ARM_PROCESS_B

columns.Name: OBJECT
columns.Comments: The main database table which will be updated by this transaction

columns.Name: OBJECT_ID
columns.Comments: The primary key of the record to be updated on the REFERENCE_TABLE

columns.Name: SUBJECT
columns.Comments: The type of the business object being worked on by this transaction, e.g. PERSON

columns.Name: SUBJECT_ID
columns.Comments: The unique identifier of the business object being worked on by this transaction, e.g. PERSON_ID

columns.Name: PARENT_TRANSACTION_ID
columns.Comments: Connection to the parent transaction record on this table

columns.Name: REENTRY_FUNCTION
columns.Comments: This column is used to store the Reentry function.

columns.Name: PROCESS_OWNER
columns.Comments: Composite Process this transaction is part of

columns.Name: SECTION_DISPLAY_NAME
columns.Comments: Display Name of the action being performed

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: ARCHIVE_DATE
columns.Comments: Indicates the date and time the transaction record was archived.

columns.Name: XML_DATA_CACHE
columns.Comments: The transaction business data and context, as XML

columns.Name: FAMILY
columns.Comments: High level grouping used in TAC searches
